bead.title: test: two concurrent workers with a stale-but-alive lease must not both execute one bead (parallel-execution regression)
bead.description:
PROBLEM: No test covers the STAGGERED contention case that actually occurred (ddx-d740539d): worker A claims and is alive but its lease aged past TTL (mid-intake / mid-long-op), and worker B then scans and reclaims -> both execute. The existing TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead only covers a same-instant race with a FRESH lease. Real-world signature: an attempt.terminated reason=bead_closed_externally event in .ddx/attachments/ddx-d740539d/events.jsonl after two attempts overlapped ~16.6 min.

ROOT CAUSE: missing end-to-end exclusivity regression for the stale-but-alive-lease scenario. This bead pins the contract that C1 (ddx-d333ad8a, intake heartbeat) + C2 (ddx-ba93de9e, PID-death takeover) must satisfy.

PROPOSED FIX: Add a chaos/integration regression simulating the exact scenario; it must FAIL before C1+C2 and PASS after.

NON-SCOPE: the fixes themselves (C1/C2).
bead.acceptance:
1. New TestChaos_StaleButAliveLeaseDoesNotPermitSecondExecution (cli/internal/agent/, new _chaos/_liveness test): worker A claims a single ready bead and enters a long phase exceeding an overridden small HeartbeatTTL while A's process is alive; concurrently run worker B's pick+claim loop against the same store. Assert EXACTLY ONE worker executes (one execute-bead attempt / one attempt_id), B never claims, and NO attempt.terminated/bead_closed_externally event is produced for the bead.
2. The test fails (two attempts) if either C1 or C2 is reverted (note this in a comment).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'TestChaos_StaleButAliveLeaseDoesNotPermitSecondExecution' -count=1 green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body:
dependencies→removed dangling ddx-ba93de9e (1 edge(s))
dependencies→removed dangling ddx-d333ad8a (1 edge(s))
